note: seq=61 (ASYNC RAMP relaunch -- own cgroup + token). The seq-59 Popen-detached ramp was killed by the autopull's cgroup cleanup and a plain standalone service lacks STATUS_TOKEN (it lives only in the autopull runtime env). This captures the STATUS_* publish creds from the autopull env into a 0600 /etc/atlas/async-ramp.env and starts atlas-async-ramp.service (its own cgroup, survives; has the token to publish). The service runs /opt/atlas/async_ramp.py (already on box) which ramps ATLAS_ASYNC_LANES 32->128->256->512, measures combined done-rate + bounded async PG conns + RSS each step, publishes status/hetzner/async-ramp.json cumulatively, PG-safe guard stops at pg_total>=90. Live fleet untouched. Allowlist-safe.

steps.note: seq=61: relaunch async ramp as own-cgroup service with publish token.

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS async lane ramp (detached, own cgroup)
After=network-online.target postgresql.service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/db.env
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/async-ramp.env
ExecStart=/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python /opt/atlas/async_ramp.py
TimeoutStartSec=1500

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import os; keys=['STATUS_TOKEN','STATUS_REPO','STATUS_API_BASE','STATUS_BRANCH','BRAIN_URL','NODE_ID']; open('/etc/atlas/async-ramp.env','w').write(''.join('%s=%s\n'%(k,os.environ[k]) for k in keys if os.environ.get(k))); os.chmod('/etc/atlas/async-ramp.env',0o600); import subprocess as s; s.run(['systemctl','daemon-reload']); s.run(['systemctl','reset-failed','atlas-async-ramp.service']); s.run(['systemctl','start','--no-block','atlas-async-ramp.service']); print('async ramp service kicked (own cgroup + token)')"
